observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that management should conduct actual inventory of all properties owned by the LGU and the corresponding report be prepared and submitted to comply with COA Circular No. 92-386. The inventory report prepared by the Treasury Office should reconcile with accounting records and necessary adjustments should be made to present a reliable balance in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The management is currently conducting inventory through identifying the actual PPEs of the agency as to the booked up value for the reconciliation to the records of the Accounting Office. Also, they are conducting inventory of unserviceable properties for the dropping of these amounts in the books of the LGU after further evaluation and processes.
observations.reason: Reiterated as Observation No. 9 in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We commended the Municipality for its substantial accomplishments on programs, projects and activities funded under the 20% Development Fund. But we have recommended that Management address the causes of delays in the implementation of the programs, projects and activities. We also recommended that Management closely monitor and ensure the implementation and completion of all the LGU’s programs, projects and activities funded under the 20% Development Fund in order to maximize the utilization of the funds and ultimately achieve desirable socio – economic development and environmental outcomes of the LGU.

We commended the Management for its effort in implementing solid waste management related activities for the betterment of the whole Municipality with a   recommendation   to   continue   encouraging   the participation of every constituent in doing its share in environmental protection and maintenance.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Treasurer’s Office personnel to intensify its collection by giving priority to the collection of the Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax Receivables which had accumulated to P34.898 million.

We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer conduct massive tax information campaign and exert efforts to ensure collection of all realty taxes.  Demand letters should be sent regularly to the delinquent owners, and if warranted, their names including the amount of their realty tax delinquencies should be posted in public places for information of everyone in the locality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the market committee to fully utilize the use of the new public market by awarding the vacant stalls to deserving applicants and institute measures to collect the arrears from market stall owners. We also recommended for the issuance of Lease Contract/MOAs to occupants of the stalls in the market.

We recommended that Management ensure the implementation and completion of all the programs, projects and activities funded under the 5% LDRRM Fund as planned so that the expected benefits to the beneficiaries/constituents will be achieved or delivered on time.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following: (a) Demand from the accountable officers to settle the unremitted collections; (b) Require strict monitoring of the accountable forms with or without money value issued to the sub-collectors; (c) no additional accountable forms will be issued to collectors unless previous accountable forms were accounted or remitted; and require the submission of monthly the report of accountability for accountable forms.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant reconciles their records regularly and reconciling items to be immediately effected as they are discovered for a fair and accurate presentation of the cash balances in the financial statements. We also recommended that individual ledger be maintained for every accountable officer by the accounting office to facilitate reconciliation of balances of each accountable officers.

We recommended that Management enforce strictly the settlement of cash advances within the prescribed period pursuant to COA Circular 97-002. We also recommended that Management stop granting additional cash advances if previous cash advances had not been liquidated and demand the liquidation of long outstanding cash advances from the Accountable Officers or withhold their salaries as prescribed under the above-cited Circular.

We recommended the Management to fully liquidate all cash advances at year end through the submission of liquidation report or refund of the remaining balances as prescribed under COA Circular 97-002 to avoid accumulation of cash advances and risk of loss thru theft and or misapplication of funds. We also recommended that Management require the liquidation of long outstanding cash advances from the accountable officers or withhold their salaries as prescribed under the above-cited Circular.

We recommended the Management to conduct an actual physical count on all Property, Plant and Equipment of the Municipality, prepare the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment and submit a copy to the Auditor’s Office to validate the existence and condition of the PPEs. We also recommended that Management maintain subsidiary ledgers for the PPEs to be reconciled with the RCPPE and reflect necessary adjustments to present a reliable balance in the financial statements.

Process the transfer of title of the lots where the Municipal Hall and the proposed Cemetery are located in the name of the LGU to protect the properties from risk of disputes or claims by other parties; and
Record in the books the lot in Luttuad, Diffun and initiate strategies to verify the details of the amount recorded in 2019 and prior years to present a reliable financial statements.

We recommended that Management require the contractor concerned to correct the defects found on the projects as recommended by the Technical Inspection Team to prevent further damage on the project and for the benefit of the constituents.  The equivalent amount of the defects maybe disallowed in audit, if warranted.

We recommended that Management strictly comply to the provisions of COA Circular 2012-001 pertaining to documentary requirements for the fund transfers to implementing agency. We also recommended that the Management issue demand letters to concerned barangays to liquidate the fund transfers and submit the liquidation reports to the Auditor’s Office. Moreover, we recommended that Municipal Accountant reconcile his records with the Bookkeeper and monitor the fund transfers given to the different barangays.

We recommended that Management enumerate specifically the Priority Infrastructure Projects in its Annual Budget to facilitate the evaluation of the projects charged against the 20% Development Fund. We also recommended that the Management submit an accomplishment report to the Auditor’s Office regarding the Priority Infrastructure Projects under the name of the Local Officials.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the reciprocal accounts and adjust the balances in the financial statements. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the reciprocal accounts on a regular basis to ensure the accuracy and reliability of the accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly adhere to the provisions of the above-cited regulations regarding the submission of accounts for timely post-audit of accounts and communication of the results to the Management.

We recommended that the Management strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 7.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and Section 100 of PD 1445 regarding submission of reports for a timely audit and communication of results.

We recommended that Management submit immediately to the Audit Team the duly accomplished AAPSI form pertaining to the audit recommendations embodied in the Annual Audit Report for CY 2020. We also recommended that management strictly adhere to the above-cited regulation by submitting the AAPSI within sixty (60) days from receipt of COA Annual Audit Report.

We recommended that Management reconcile the Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities to Surplus/(Deficit) and disclosed it either in the Statement of Cash Flows or in the Notes to the Financial Statements for a fair presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended that Management reconcile their records with the Audit Team. We also recommended that the Management exert all available remedies in enforcing the immediate settlement of all audit disallowances pursuant to pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and to be more prudent in the processing of claims for payments to avoid audit disallowances and suspensions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Treasurer’s Office personnel to intensify its collection by giving priority to the collection of the real property tax receivables which had accumulated to P32.889 million.

We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer conduct massive tax information campaign and exert efforts to ensure collection of all realty taxes.  Demand letters should be sent regularly to the delinquent owners, and if warranted, their names including the amount of their realty tax delinquencies should be posted in public places for information of everyone in the locality.
observations.managementAction: The Management issued certificate of RPT delinquency to those owners with delinquent realty taxes. The tax information campaign were not conducted due to the COVID-19 pandemic. Lastly, the problem of the agency was that the listed owners were not the current actual owners of the property.
observations.reason: Reiterated as Observation No. 1 in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the market committee to fully utilize the use of the new public market by awarding the vacant stalls to deserving applicants and institute measures to collect the arrears from market stall owners. We also recommended for the issuance of Lease contract/MOAs to occupants of the stalls in the market.
observations.managementAction: The Management created a committee for the inventory of current stall owners in the market, for the reason that some occupants were not the one who were with lease contract with the municipality. Management commented that the real problem was the stall owners who were in MOA with the LGU were renting out the stalls to other occupants. With regards to the uncollected rentals with penalties and interest of the old building, the Municipal Treasurer suggested that they will condone penalties for the active stall owners with the concurrence of the Sanggunian so as to encourage settlement to those delinquent stall owners.
observations.reason: Reiterated as Observation No. 2 in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the management ensure the implementation and completion of all the municipality’s programs, projects and activities funded under the 5% LDRRM Fund so that the expected benefits due the beneficiaries/constituents shall be achieved or delivered on time.
observations.managementAction: Management commented that there were other available funds such as the Bayanihan Grant for Municipalities (BGCM) fund for the municipality to utilized in line with mitigation activities in response to the COVID-19 pandemic. The low utilization of the QRF was due to the fact that the agency is still saving the fund for the latter part of the year knowing that those were the times where typhoons will strike the country. The LDRRMO also said that the QRF fund were already utilized in 2021 in response to the rapid increase of covid-19 positive cases in the municipality.

The Management already withhold salaries of the accountable officers with shortages. They already issued a memo regarding the settlement of the shortages to the concerned AOs and an appeal has been received from the latter stating that they should reconsider the. The Local Chief Executive has issued a memorandum on those accountable officers to remit their collections and deposit the same to the agency’s depository bank.
observations.reason: Reiterated as Observation No. 4 in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive enforce the settlement of cash advances due from all officials and employees of the Municipality. Stop granting additional cash advances if previous cash advances had not been liquidated. Effect the withholding of salaries of the officials and employees concerned and impose sanctions, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: Management issued demand letters to Accountable Officers with outstanding cash advances.
observations.reason: Reiterated as Observation No. 7 in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that all cash advances granted be fully liquidated at year end through the submission of liquidation report or refund the remaining balances. We also recommended that management employ the payroll thru Automated Teller Machine (ATM) on all payments of salaries and wages to all municipal personnel to avoid accumulation of cash advances and risk of loss thru theft and or misapplication of funds.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent to Officers and employees with unliquidated balances.
observations.reason: Reiterated as Observation No. 8 in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following to the Management:

a.	Create a committee or group that will primarily handle the inventory of local road network system and in coordination with the Municipal Engineer and Municipal GSO/Treasurer to complete the inventory of all local roads and road map and Local Road Network Property Cards; submit the inventory report with complete description and cost segregation of road components to the Municipal Accountant;

b.	The LGU shall maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map and shall keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and

c.	The Municipal Accountant shall prepare the Journal Voucher to record the inventory of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card; keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network, and; ensure that 100% of the value of road network of the Municipality shall be booked-up in 2019 consistent with COA Circular No. 2015-008. Full disclosure on road networks should be made in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer said that there were many unnamed roads in the municipality especially those on the far-flung barangays, thus, inventory of roads cannot be undertaken. Complete data on roads were undertaken for the year 2017 up to present only. Also, non-reconciliation on the booked-up value on the records in the Accounting Office and the records of the Engineering office were the hindrance in taking the inventory on road networks.
observations.reason: Reiterated as Observation No. 10 in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Local Chief Executive and the concerned department to initiate some strategies to collect the receivable from the debtor; verify the details of the account from old records for possible issuance of demand letters; and if after exhausting all remedies, the account cannot be collected anymore, file a request to the Commission on Audit for the accounts to be written off.
observations.reason: Reiterated as Observation No. 13 in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated our recommendation that management comply with the provisions of PPSAS 12 relative to the recording of procured supplies as inventory and recognized only as expense upon actual consumption based on the Supplies Adjustment Report submitted by the requisitioning office.

We recommended that Management adhere with the pertinent provisions of the Local Budget Circular No. 125 dated April 4, 2020 on the recording, reporting and utilization of the Bayanihan Grant to the Municipality.

We recommended the management to use as reference the issuances of the DOH, DTI and other government agencies relative to procurement of goods relative to prevention of COVID19.  Prior to any further audit action, we recommend that Management provide concrete explanation on the above observations.  Refund the amount if warranted to avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommended that management issue a demand letter to concerned barangays to submit the fund utilization report together with inspection report, photos and certificate of project completion rendered by the agency for proper recording/adjustments in the books. Management should also request the Barangays to return the excess amount on the completed projects in compliance with Item 3 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant already sent demand letters to the concerned barangay captains.
observations.reason: Reiterated as Observation No. 14 in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management (a) establish clear-cut guidelines for the grant of communication allowances to officials and employees; and (b) consider adopting the post-paid plans offered by the network providers for economic reasons and to foster equity in the grant of allowances in relation to actual communication needs of the LGU. We also recommend that grant to allowances and benefits to other national government officials be stop until the full implementation of the standardization law has been granted to the employees of the municipality. A notice of suspension and disallowance shall be issued when warranted.

We have recommended that management strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 7.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and Sections 39 (1) and 100 of PD 1445.  The prompt submission of accounts and transactions will enable the auditor to conduct a timely audit and could recommend measures on deficiencies noted, if any.
observations.reason: Reiterated as Observation No. 18 in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the management to ensure that all actual expenditures under GAD fund are within the approved GAD budget and ensure that the funds are used for projects and activities that are related to empowerment of women and children as required by laws, rules and regulations.

We commended management on its effort in settlement of the audit disallowances and recommended settlement of the remaining audit disallowances pursuant to Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and to be more prudent in the processing of claims for payments to avoid audit disallowances and suspensions.
observations.reason: Reiterated as Observation No. 22 in Part II of this Report.

observations.managementAction: The amount of P6,908,436.96 was reduced by P744,624.86 or 10.78% reduction as of December, 2020. Done daily monitoring of collections and deposit and Memo was already issued for withholding salaries of RCC with outstanding unremitted collections. However, the withheld salaries of RCCs with unremitted collections will not suffice the accumulated amount of unremitted as audited.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following to the Management:

The Municipal Engineer shall provide the Municipal Accountant with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects;

The LGU shall maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map and shall keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and

The Municipal Accountant shall prepare the Journal Voucher to record the inventory of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card; keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network, and; ensure that 100% of the value of road network of the Municipality shall be booked-up in 2019 consistent with COA Circular No. 2015-008. Full disclosure on road networks should be made in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Inventory of Road Network for 2018 and 2019 was furnished to Accounting Office. However, Journal Voucher and Subsidiary Ledger for road network is not maintained separately.

observations.managementAction: EO was issued for reconstitution of Market Committee; conducted stalls inspection and inventory and reports were communicated to LCE; issued Notice of Delinquencies. There is a slim possibility of collecting the accumulated market stall rentals since 1996 to 2019 amounting to P3.1M because previous stall owners in the Old Buildings are nowhere to be found.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Treasurer’s Office personnel to intensify its collection by giving priority to the collection of the real property tax receivables which had accumulated to P38.536M.

We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer conduct massive tax information campaign and exert efforts to ensure collection of all realty taxes.  Demand letters should be sent regularly to the delinquent owners, and if warranted, their names including the amount of their realty tax delinquencies should be posted in public places for information of everyone in the locality.
observations.managementAction: Tax information and collection campaign was conducted dated February 6 to April 02, 2020. Also, Notice of Tax Delinquencies has been prepared for CY 2019 for individual real property taxpayers. However, the RPT receivable had still an outstanding balance of P32.889M as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management fast track the implementation of the priority projects funded by the national government agencies. The remaining or unutilized amount on programs/projects which were already been completed should be returned to the source agencies.
observations.managementAction: The management had already submitted liquidation report

observations.recommendation: We commended the LGU for its significant projects and activities on disaster risk reduction and management and in providing assistance and humanitarian response to affected communities. Also, we recommended that management: (a) ensure the implementation and completion of all the municipality’s programs, projects and activities funded under the 5% LDRRM Fund so that the expected benefits due the beneficiaries/constituents shall be achieved or delivered on time.
observations.managementAction: The utilization of the LDRRM fund was still not maximized with only 40.97% utilization rate for CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to monitor the accountable forms issued to the sub-collectors and no additional accountable forms should be issued if the previous accountable forms were not accounted or remitted. Furthermore, Settlement of the said unremitted and unpresented collections should be strictly adhered. Also, we recommended the management to require all accountable officers to adhere strictly with the regulation and submit monthly the report of accountability for accountable forms.
observations.managementAction: The MTO had already the control of all accountable forms in their possession which has started January, 2020. Also, logbook of AF had been maintained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to require all the collecting officers to maintain a cash book to record their accountabilities. Also, at the end of the month or when required to do so by proper competent authority, the Accountable Officer (AO) shall rule and foot the cashbook and certify the balances as required by COA Memorandum No. 2013-004 or the Revised Cash Examination Manual. Lastly, adherence to the Manual on NGAS for LGUs is highly enjoined.
observations.managementAction: Instructions were given by the MTO to all revenue collectors to maintain their individual CASHBOOK. Memo was issued to that effect.

Stop granting additional cash advances if previous cash advances had not been liquidated. Effect the withholding of salaries of the officials and employees concerned.  Impose sanctions, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent to Officers and employees demanding them to settle their cash advances. Request letter for write off CA of retired employees, died and whose whereabouts can no longer be located was filed in COA office.

observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent to Officers and employees demanding them to settle their CA last October 26,2020.                                      175 regular employees are having LBP ATM cards for the payment of salaries and other benefits.

observations.managementAction: Submission of some accounts were still not within the reglementary period

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that:

a.	Management particularly the Accounting Office strictly comply with the existing rules and regulations stated above in the preparation and approval of the payrolls to avoid sanctions that may be imposed for non-compliance thereof. 

b.	In the event that the total authorized deductions reduce the net take home pay to less than P5,000.00, the authorized deductions for BIR, GSIS, HDMF and PHIC shall enjoy first preference, those deductions for mutual benefits association, thrift banks and savings and loan association shall enjoy second preference and so forth. The other deductions that are not accommodated during the pay period should already be paid directly by the employee concerned to the loaning institution.
observations.managementAction: Strict monitoring of Net take home pay of regular employees had been adhered in compliance with Section 52 of General Appropriations Act.

observations.recommendation: We commended management on its effort in settlement of the audit disallowances and recommended settlement of the remaining audit disallowances pursuant to Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and to be more prudent in the processing of claims for payments to avoid audit disallowances and suspensions.
observations.managementAction: Reconcile the balance amounting to P6,486.95.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the General Services Officer/Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Engineer on the identification of local road networks and comply with the proper recording of the infrastructure assets in the books of accounts and the necessary schedules and property ledgers should be maintained for proper monitoring thereof.
observations.managementAction: Management had not yet implemented the recommendations. There were no disclosure in the notes in the financial statements regarding the Road Network system.
observations.reason: Reiterated under finding no. 10

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Liquidating Officer to deposit all collections as required by rules and regulation to prevent possible misappropriation, theft and loss of funds. Impose administrative sanctions if warranted.
observations.managementAction: Memorandum Order dated December 2 and 27, 2019 were already issued to revenue collectors for the remittance of their collection. However, the account had still an outstanding balance as of December 31, 2019, representing the undeposited collections of the collectors.
observations.reason: Reiterated under finding no. 5

We also recommended that management employ the payroll thru Automated Teller Machine (ATM) on all payments of salaries and wages to all municipal personnel.
observations.managementAction: Cash advances granted as of December 31, 2018 had already been liquidated. However, outstanding cash advances were still unliquidated as of December 31, 2019 representing the Advances for Operating expenses, Payroll and to Special Disbursing Officer.
observations.reason: Reiterated under finding no. 9

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the concerned department to initiate some strategies to collect the receivable from the debtors. Verify the details of the account from old records for issuance of demand letters. Request the accounts to be written off if after exhausting all remedies, the account cannot be collected anymore.

observations.reason: Reiterated under Finding no. 11

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated our recommendation that management comply with the provisions of PPSAS 12 relative to the recording of procured supplies as inventory and recognized only as expense upon actual consumption based on the Supplies Adjustment Report submitted by the requisitioning office.
observations.managementAction: Procured supplies were still directly charged to expense accounts upon purchase.
observations.reason: Reiterated under finding no. 15

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that:

a.	Management particularly the Accounting Office strictly comply with the existing rules and regulations stated above in the preparation and approval of the payrolls to avoid sanctions that may be imposed for non-compliance thereof. 

b.	In the event that the total authorized deductions reduce the net take home pay to less than P4,000.00, the authorized deductions for BIR, GSIS, HDMF and PHIC shall enjoy first preference, those deductions for mutual benefits association, thrift banks and savings and loan association shall enjoy second preference and so forth. The other deductions that are not accommodated during the pay period should already be paid directly by the employee concerned to the loaning institution. 

c.	The Municipal Accountant exercise diligence in certifying the monthly net take home pay of employees and provide emergency measures to assuage the situation of those who have been negatively affected by their loan deductions.
observations.managementAction: There were still employees with a monthly net take home pay below the minimum requirement.
observations.reason: Reiterated under finding no.16

observations.recommendation:
We recommended  the Local Chief Executive instruct the market committee to fully utilize the use of the new public market by awarding the vacant stalls to deserving applicants and institute measures to collect the arrears from market stall owners.
We also recommended for the issuance of Lease contract/MOAs to occupants of the stalls in the market.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Treasurer’s Office personnel to intensify its collection by giving priority to the collection of the real property tax receivables which had accumulated to P38.536M.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer conduct massive tax information campaign and exert efforts to ensure collection of all realty taxes.  Demand letters should be sent regularly to the delinquent owners, and if warranted, their names including the amount of their realty tax delinquencies should be posted in public places for information of everyone in the locality.

We recommended that Management fast track the implementation of the priority projects funded by the national government agencies. The remaining or unutilized amount on programs/projects which were already been completed should be returned to the source agencies.

(a) ensure the implementation and completion of all the municipality’s programs, projects and activities funded under the 5% LDRRM Fund so that the expected benefits due the beneficiaries/constituents shall be achieved or delivered on time.

We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to monitor the accountable forms issued to the sub-collectors and no additional accountable forms should be issued if the previous accountable forms were not accounted or remitted. Furthermore, Settlement of the said unremitted and unpresented collections should be strictly adhered. Also, we recommended the management to require all accountable officers to adhere strictly with the regulation and submit monthly the report of accountability for accountable forms.

We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to require all the collecting officers to maintain a cash book to record their accountabilities. Also, at the end of the month or when required to do so by proper competent authority, the Accountable Officer (AO) shall rule and foot the cashbook and certify the balances as required by COA Memorandum No. 2013-004 or the Revised Cash Examination Manual. Lastly, adherence to the Manual on NGAS for LGUs is highly enjoined.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive again enforce the settlement of cash advances due from all officials and employees of the Municipality. Stop granting additional cash advances if previous cash advances had not been liquidated. Effect the withholding of salaries of the officials and employees concerned.  Impose sanctions, if warranted.

We reiterated our recommendation that all cash advances granted be fully liquidated at year end through the submission of liquidation report or refund the remaining balances. We also recommended that management employ the payroll thru Automated Teller Machine (ATM) on all payments of salaries and wages to all municipal personnel.

We recommended the following to the Management:
The Municipal Engineer shall provide the Municipal Accountant with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects;
The LGU shall maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map and shall keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and
The Municipal Accountant shall prepare the Journal Voucher to record the inventory of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card; keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network, and; ensure that 100% of the value of road network of the Municipality shall be booked-up in 2019 consistent with COA Circular No. 2015-008. Full disclosure on road networks should be made in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the LGU.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Local Chief Executive and the concerned department to initiate some strategies to collect the receivable from the debtor; verify the details of the account from old records for possible issuance of demand letters; and if after exhausting all remedies, the account cannot be collected anymore, file a request to the Commission on Audit for the accounts to be written off.
We reiterate our previous recommendation that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the balances of the reciprocal accounts regularly to present a reliable balances of the accounts.

We reiterated our recommendation that management should conduct actual inventory of all properties owned by the LGU and the corresponding report be prepared and submitted to comply with COA Circular No. 92-386. The inventory report prepared by the Treasury Office should reconcile with accounting records and necessary adjustments should be made to present a reliable balance in the financial statements.

We have recommended that management strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 7.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and Sections 39 (1) and 100 of PD 1445.  The prompt submission of accounts and transactions will enable the auditor to conduct a timely audit and could recommend measures on deficiencies noted, if any.

We have reiterated our recommendation that management comply with the provisions of PPSAS 12 relative to the recording of procured supplies as inventory and recognized only as expense upon actual consumption based on the Supplies Adjustment Report submitted by the requisitioning office.

We reiterated our recommendation that:
Management particularly the Accounting Office strictly comply with the existing rules and regulations stated above in the preparation and approval of the payrolls to avoid sanctions that may be imposed for non-compliance thereof.
In the event that the total authorized deductions reduce the net take home pay to less than P5,000.00, the authorized deductions for BIR, GSIS, HDMF and PHIC shall enjoy first preference, those deductions for mutual benefits association, thrift banks and savings and loan association shall enjoy second preference and so forth. The other deductions that are not accommodated during the pay period should already be paid directly by the employee concerned to the loaning institution.
The Municipal Accountant exercise diligence in certifying the monthly net take home pay of employees and provide emergency measures to assuage the situation of those who have been negatively affected by their loan deductions.

We commended management on its effort in settlement of the audit disallowances and recommended settlement of the remaining audit disallowances pursuant to Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and to be more prudent in the processing of claims for payments to avoid audit disallowances and suspensions.

We commended the management for the compliance with the statutory obligations of the municipality through remittance of the mandatory deductions due to other government entities. However, we recommended the Municipal Accountant to make proper reconciliations for the abnormal balance of the Due to GOCC’s account.

We reiterated our recommendation that management: (a) ensure the implementation and completion of all the LGU’s programs, projects and activities funded under the 20% Development Fund so that the expected benefits due the beneficiaries/constituents shall be achieved or delivered on time; and (b) Improve the planning and scheduling of work activities and manpower in order to facilitate completion of program of work of identified projects.
observations.managementAction: Various bidding was conducted as early as 1st quarter to ensure the full implementation of different development projects. Also, 33 barangay project fund amounting to P6.6M were transferred/downloaded to their barangay accounts amounting to P200,000.00 each. Lastly, As of first semester, all the unfinished projects were already done.

331 Lease Contracts/MOA was awarded to stall owners and 103 demand letters were issued to delinquent stall owners, thus resulting to substantial decrease in arrears from stall rentals from P16.781 million to P3.430 million.
Massive tax information campaign was conducted last January 2019. Notice of tax delinquencies were also disseminated to 33 barangays.
observations.reason: Reiterated under finding no 2.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management implement the priority projects funded by the national government agencies and monitor the utilization of funds by ensuring compliance with the memorandum of agreement with the source agency. Likewise, the remaining or unutilized amount on programs/projects which already have been completed should be returned to the source agency.
observations.managementAction: Projects funded by the national government agencies for the year 2018 were fully implemented      as of December 31, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant record the proper asset account for completed projects funded by national government agencies. Coordinate with the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and General Services Officer endeavor to identify all completed and on-going projects funded by other national government agencies for the proper recognition in the books of accounts of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Completed projects funded by the National Government were recognized and recorded in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the payment of current obligations with valid claims that are supported with complete documentations; and to recommend to the Sangguniang Bayan the reversion of undocumented and dormant payables without valid claimants.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to management the following: (a) Require that the gender issues and concerns prevailing among the women constituents be identified; (b) Map out the GAD projects/activities for the year and the corresponding specific strategies to address the issues; (c) Review and evaluate the results; (d) The Accomplishment report should be prepared using the required form, to document the extent of implementation, the efficiency and effectiveness of the GAD projects and activities.
observations.managementAction: A total amount of P9,916,193.91 were spent for GAD responsive project, programs and activities.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

a.	Management particularly the Accounting Office strictly comply with the existing rules and regulations stated above in the preparation and approval of the payrolls to avoid sanctions that may be imposed for non-compliance thereof. 

b.	In the event that the total authorized deductions reduce the net      take home pay to less than P4,000.00, the authorized deductions for BIR, GSIS, HDMF and PHIC shall enjoy first preference, those deductions for mutual benefits association, thrift banks and savings and loan association   shall enjoy second preference and so forth. The other deductions   that are not accommodated during the     pay period     should already be paid directly by the employee concerned to the loaning institution. 

c.	The Municipal Accountant exercise diligence in certifying the monthly net take home pay of employees  and provide emergency measures to assuage the situation of those who have been negatively affected by their loan deductions.
observations.managementAction: Management had exercise due diligence in certifying the net take home of several employees who are in need of loan. However, there were still employees with a monthly net take home pay of P4,000.00 and below.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that management: (a) ensure the implementation and completion of all the LGU’s programs, projects and activities funded under the 20% Development Fund so that the expected benefits due the beneficiaries/constituents shall be achieved or delivered on time; and (b) Improve the planning and scheduling of work activities and manpower in order to facilitate completion of program of work of identified projects.

We recommended that management implement the priority projects funded by the national government agencies and monitor the utilization of funds by ensuring compliance with the memorandum of agreement with the source agency. Likewise, the remaining or unutilized amount on programs/projects which already have been completed should be returned to the source agency.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the General Services Officer/Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Engineer on the identification of local road networks and comply with the proper recording of the infrastructure assets in the books of accounts and the necessary schedules and property ledgers should be maintained for proper monitoring thereof.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Liquidating Officer to deposit all collections as required by rules and regulation to prevent possible misappropriation, theft and loss of funds. Impose administrative sanctions if warranted.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive again enforce the settlement of cash advances due from all officials and employees of the Municipality. Stop granting additional cash advances if previous cash advances had not been liquidated. Effect the withholding of salaries of the officials and employees concerned.  Impose sanctions, if warranted.

We reiterated our recommendation that all cash advances granted be fully liquidated at year end through the submission of liquidation report or refund the remaining balances. We also recommended that management employ the payroll thru Automated Teller Machine (ATM) on all payments of salaries and wages to all municipal personnel.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the concerned department to initiate some strategies to collect the receivable from the debtors. Verify the details of the account from old records for issuance of demand letters. Request the accounts to be written off if after exhausting all remedies, the account cannot be collected anymore.
We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the balances of the reciprocal accounts regularly to present a reliable balances of the accounts.

We reiterated our  recommendation that management  conduct actual inventory of all properties owned by the LGU and the corresponding report be prepared and submitted to comply with COA Circular No. 92-386. The inventory report prepared by the Treasury Office should reconcile with accounting records and necessary adjustments should be made to present a reliable balance in the financial statements.

We have recommended that management strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 7.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and Sections 39 (1) and 100 of PD 1445.  The prompt submission of accounts and transactions will enable the auditor to conduct a timely audit and could recommend measures on deficiencies noted, if any.

We have reiterated our recommendation that management comply with the provisions of PPSAS 12 relative to the recording of procured supplies as inventory and recognized only as expense upon actual consumption based on the Supplies Adjustment Report submitted by the requisitioning office.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant record the proper asset account for completed projects funded by national government agencies. Coordinate with the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and General Services Officer endeavor to identify all completed and on-going projects funded by other national government agencies for the proper recognition in the books of accounts of the Municipality.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the payment of current obligations with valid claims that are supported with complete documentations; and to recommend to the Sangguniang Bayan the reversion of undocumented and dormant payables without valid claimants.

We commended the management for supporting RA 9003 also known as the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000. However, we recommended that the utilization of funds appropriated for environmental programs and activities should be maximized for the betterment of the whole municipality.

We recommended to management the following: (a) Require that the gender issues and concerns prevailing among the women constituents be identified; (b) Map out the GAD projects/activities for the year and the corresponding specific strategies to address the issues; (c) Review and evaluate the results; (d) The Accomplishment report should be prepared using the required form, to document the extent of implementation, the efficiency and effectiveness of the GAD projects and activities.
However, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned offices to plan and implement programs that are directly attributable to the benefit of children.

We reiterated our recommendation that:
Management particularly the Accounting Office strictly comply with the existing rules and regulations stated above in the preparation and approval of the payrolls to avoid sanctions that may be imposed for non-compliance thereof.
In the event that the total authorized deductions reduce the net take home pay to less than P4,000.00, the authorized deductions for BIR, GSIS, HDMF and PHIC shall enjoy first preference, those deductions for mutual benefits association, thrift banks and savings and loan association shall enjoy second preference and so forth. The other deductions that are not accommodated during the pay period should already be paid directly by the employee concerned to the loaning institution.
The Municipal Accountant exercise diligence in certifying the monthly net take home pay of employees and provide emergency measures to assuage the situation of those who have been negatively affected by their loan deductions.
However, we recommended the Municipal Accountant to make proper reconciliations for the abnormal balance of the Due to GOCC’s account.

We commended management on its effort in settlement of the audit disallowances and recommended settlement of the remaining audit disallowances pursuant to Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and to be more prudent in the processing of claims for payments to avoid audit disallowances and suspensions.

We recommended that management: (a) ensure the implementation and completion of all the LGU’s programs, projects and activities funded under the 20% Development Fund so that the expected benefits due the beneficiaries/constituents shall be achieved or delivered on time; and (b) Improve the planning and scheduling of work activities and manpower in order to facilitate completion of program of work of identified projects.
observations.managementAction: The unimplemented programs under 20% Development fund for CY 2017 has been implemented for the year 2018. However, there were still unimplemented projects for the year ended under the fund which were programmed for CY 2018.

Likewise, the remaining or unutilized amount on programs/projects which already have been completed should be returned to the source agency.
observations.managementAction: All BUB and ADM projects have been fully implemented during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require      the Municipal Accountant to prepare   the complete bank reconciliation statements    for all bank accounts    of the LGU to be able to identify reconciling items and possible erroneous recordings and mispostings of transactions in the books and the banks. Adherence to the provision of COA Circular No. 96-011 and Section 74 of P.D. 1445 is required.
observations.managementAction: Bank Reconciliation Statements for the trust fund accounts were already prepared and submitted to the office for further verification of the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require all collectors to regularly remit all their collections to the Municipal Treasurer who will deposit the same to authorized government depository bank to avoid misapplication of government funds. Furthermore, we recommended all accountable officers to prepare the Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms in compliance to Section 50 of NGAS for LGUs.

Also, the balance of P5,186,855.31 also includes balances from the past Municipal Treasurers. Reconciliation of the account is still on-going and deposits have been made at the first quarter of 2018 pertaining to the undeposited collections.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the balances of the reciprocal accounts regularly to present a reliable balances of the accounts.

We recommended that management allocate the 5% fund based on the income from local sources and transfer/deposit said amount as trust fund for LDRRM.  We also recommended that subsidiary ledgers as well as bank reconciliation statements are prepared for the particular trust fund account.
We recommended that management submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the formats prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-002 on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRM Council and LDC to the COA auditor.

We commended the management for supporting RA 9003 also known as the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000. However, we recommended that the utilization of funds appropriated for environmental programs and activities should be maximized for the betterment of the whole municipality.

We recommended the management to; allocate at least five (5) percent of the Municipality’s total appropriation for the GAD;   ensure that the GAD Fund are disbursed for projects and activities that are related to empowerment of women and children programs and activities; and prepare accomplishment reports based on the GAD Plan and Budget.
We recommended that management use the fund strictly  in accordance with the provisions of the RA 5447 or SEF Law and Section 272 of RA 7160.

We commended the management for its compliance to DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 and Section 15 of RA 9344 also known as “Juvenile Justice and Welfare Act of 2006”.  However, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned offices to plan and implement programs that are directly attributable to the benefit of children.

We recommended the following:
Management particularly the Accounting Office strictly comply with the existing rules and regulations stated above in the preparation and approval of the payrolls to avoid sanctions that may be imposed for non-compliance thereof.
In the event that the total authorized deductions reduce the net take home pay to less than P4,000.00, the authorized deductions for BIR, GSIS, HDMF and PHIC shall enjoy first preference, those deductions for mutual benefits association, thrift banks and savings and loan association shall enjoy second preference and so forth. The other deductions that are not accommodated during the pay period should already be paid directly by the employee concerned to the loaning institution.
The Municipal Accountant exercise diligence in certifying the monthly net take home pay of employees and provide emergency measures to assuage the situation of those who have been negatively affected by their loan deductions.

We commended the management for the compliance with the statutory obligations of the municipality through remittance of the mandatory deductions due to other government entities. However, we recommended the Municipal Accountant to make proper reconciliations for the abnormal balance of the Due to GOCC’s account.

We reiterated our recommendation that the management exert effort in the immediate settlement of all audit disallowances pursuant to Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and to be more prudent in the processing of claims for payments to avoid audit disallowances and suspensions.

We recommended that management fully utilize the use of the new public market by awarding the vacant stalls to deserving applicants to increase the income of the municipality as an exercise of its revenue-raising power under the Local Government Code. We also recommended for the issuance of MOAs before allowing occupation of the stalls in the market. Likewise for the management to exert extra effort  in the collection of all stall rental delinquencies, and delinquent stall owners should be least priority in the awarding of stalls of the new market.
observations.managementAction: There is already partial utilization of the public market and stalls has been awarded to deserving applicant owner. MOA between stall owners and the LGU has already been signed.
observations.reason: Reiterated with some modifications under finding no. 2

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Focal Person closely monitor the implementation of all BUB projects to attain its purpose of poverty reduction. Coordination with available trainers not only from TESDA but from other TESDA-accredited agencies should be done to avoid delay in carrying out the programs. Limit the withdrawal of cash up to the  actual amount needed  per project implementation and not withdrawn at once to avoid cash build up  with  the accountable officer and possible misapplication/loss.
observations.managementAction: The non-implementation of projects was due to the delayed preparation of documents for public bidding.
observations.reason: Reiterated with some modifications under finding no. 4.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant issue demand letters to all active officials and employees with outstanding cash advances as of December 31, 2016 and initiate the request for write-off and/or adjustment of account balances for accountable officers who have already died consistent with COA Circular No. 2016-005. Strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 is enjoined.
observations.managementAction: Some officials and employees are already submitting partially their liquidation of cash advances. Demand letters are already been issued and request for write-off will be submitted for those employees who were already dead and nowhere to be found.
observations.reason: Reiterated with some modifications under finding no. 7.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that all cash advances granted be fully liquidated at year end through the submission of liquidation report or refund of the un-utilized balances. Comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the liquidation of cash advances. We also recommended that management employ the payroll tru Automated Teller Machine (ATM) on all payments of salaries and wages to all municipal personnel.
observations.managementAction: Most of the balances pertains to trust fund that will be given to beneficiaries such as pensions, and the like
observations.reason: Reiterated with some modifications under finding no. 8.

observations.recommendation: We  recommended  that the Municipal Accountant  reconcile the balances of reciprocal accounts so that only the net amount shall be reflected in the financial statements.
observations.reason: Reiterated with some modifications under finding no. 14.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the previous year audit recommendations that management conduct actual inventory of all properties owned by the LGU and the corresponding report be prepared and submitted to comply with COA Circular No. 92-386. The inventory report prepared by the GSO should reconcile with accounting records and necessary adjustments should be made to present a reliable balance in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Appraisal Committee will conduct an actual inventory of the Property, Plant and Equipment of the municipality.
observations.reason: Reiterated with some modifications under finding no. 11.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make a careful perusal of the aforementioned accounts payables with the view of recommending to the Local Chief Executive and the Legislative body for the reversion of the same to the Government Equity Account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant will reverse the accounts payable account to government equity.
observations.reason: Reiterated with some modifications under finding no. 13.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management fully monitor the compliance of the LGU to government regulations to avoid paying penalites and the negative effect to the performance of the municipality. The Sanitary land fill should be opened immediately to comply with the provisions of RA 9003.  The utilization of funds appropriated for environmental programs and activities should be maximized in order to attain the objective  of ensuring protection of public health and the environment in the municipality.
observations.managementAction: The necessary documentary requirements will be secured.

observations.recommendation: We maintain our previous year’s recommendation that the management strictly comply with the prescribed rules and regulations governing Gender and Development.
observations.managementAction: GAD Plan was already prepared and submitted
observations.reason: Reiterated with some modifications under finding no. 18.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the head of the Barangay Health Office and the Barangay Officials concerned make proper representation with the Municipal Government, up to the Provincial Government, as conditioned by necessity, for the improvement of the BHU facilities and to address concerns of patients and health workers. Accessibility of the health centers should be considered in choosing the location before construction to avoid wastage of fund in case of non-utilization of health centers.  Ensure that the basic services needed by the people should be delivered pursuant to the responsibilities laid down by the Local Government Code.
observations.managementAction: Dialogues and representations are being initiated for funding of necessary needed supplies and materials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the public market be fully utilized to provide additional income to the municipality which will also augment the amortization for the repayment of said loans.
observations.managementAction: Dialogues are being conducted to prospective occupants of stalls
observations.reason: Reiterated with some modifications under finding no. 27.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer make strategies, conduct massive tax information campaign and exert efforts to ensure collection of all realty taxes.  Demand letters should be sent regularly to the delinquent owners, and if warranted, their names including the amount of their realty tax delinquencies should be posted in public places so that the public may know who they are.
observations.managementAction: Dissemination of Notice/demand letter on delinquent taxpayers has been conducted to different barangay. P1.332 Million has been collected partially on delinquent taxpayers out of the collection efficiency of P3.650Million.
observations.reason: Reiterated with some modifications under finding no. 3.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the management exert effort in the immediate settlement of all audit disallowances pursuant to Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and to be more prudent in the processing of claims for payments to avoid audit disallowances and suspensions.
observations.managementAction: Salary deductions are regularly being done to settle disallowances
observations.reason: Reiterated with some modifications under finding no. 26.

observations.managementAction: No action. Cash advances remain unliquidated at year end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management fully utilize the use of the new public market by awarding the vacant stalls to deserving applicants to increase the income of the municipality as an exercise of its revenue-raising power under the Local Government Code. We also recommended for the issuance of MOAs before allowing occupation of the stalls in the market. Likewise for the management to exert extra effort  in the collection of all stall rental delinquencies, and delinquent stall owners should be least priority in the awarding of stalls of the new market.

We recommended that the Focal Person  closely monitor the implementation of all BUB projects to attain its purpose of poverty reduction. Coordination with available trainers not only from TESDA but from other TESDA-accredited agencies should be done to avoid delay in carrying out the programs. Limit the withdrawal of cash up to the  actual amount needed  per project implementation and not withdrawn at once to avoid cash build up  with  the accountable officer and possible misapplication/loss.

We reiterated our past years’ audit recommendation that the management  require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the complete bank reconciliation statements to be able to identify reconciling items and possible erroneous recordings and mispostings of transactions in the books and the banks. Adherence to the provision of COA Circular No. 96-011 and Section 74 of P.D. 1445 is required.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant issue demand letters to all active officials and employees with outstanding cash advances as of December 31, 2016 and initiate the request for write-off and/or adjustment of account balances for accountable officers who have already died consistent with COA Circular No. 2016-005. Strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 is enjoined.

We recommended that all cash advances granted be fully liquidated at year end through the submission of liquidation report or refund of the un-utilized balances. Comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the liquidation of cash advances. We also recommended that management employ the payroll tru Automated Teller Machine (ATM) on all payments of salaries and wages to all municipal personnel.

We reiterated our recommendation that the management submit a written request to  COA for an authority to write-off from the books of the LGU the recorded amounts of these Receivable accounts consistent with COA Circular No. 2016-005.
We  recommended  that the Municipal Accountant  reconcile the balances of reciprocal accounts so that only the net amount shall be reflected in the financial statements.

We reiterated the previous year audit recommendations that management  conduct actual inventory of all properties owned by the LGU and the corresponding report be prepared and submitted to comply with COA Circular No. 92-386. The inventory report prepared by the GSO should reconcile with accounting records and necessary adjustments should be made to present a reliable balance in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make a careful perusal of the aforementioned accounts payables with the view of recommending to the Local Chief Executive and the Legislative body for the reversion  of the same to the Government Equity Account.

We recommended that management fully monitor the compliance of the LGU to government regulations to avoid paying penalites and the negative effect to the performance of the municipality. The Sanitary land fill should be opened immediately to comply with the provisions of RA 9003.  The utilization of funds appropriated for environmental programs and activities should be maximized in order to attain the objective  of ensuring protection of public health and the environment in the municipality.
We maintain our previous year’s recommendation that the management strictly comply with the prescribed rules and regulations governing Gender and Development.

We recommended that the  head of the Barangay Health Office and the Barangay Officials concerned  make proper representation with the Municipal Government, up to the Provincial Government, as conditioned by necessity, for the improvement of the BHU facilities and to address concerns of patients and health workers. Accessibility of the  health centers should be considered in choosing the location before construction to avoid wastage of fund in case of non-utilization of health centers.  Ensure that the basic services needed by the people should be delivered pursuant to the responsibilities laid down by the Local Government Code.
We recommended that the public market be fully utilized to provide additional income to the municipality which will also augment the amortization for the repayment of said loans.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer  make strategies, conduct massive tax information campaign and exert efforts to ensure collection of all realty taxes.  Demand letters should be sent regularly to the delinquent owners, and if warranted, their names including the amount of their realty tax delinquencies should be posted in public places so that the public may know who they are.

We reiterated our recommendation that the management exert effort in the immediate settlement of all audit disallowances pursuant to Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and to be more prudent in the processing of claims for payments to avoid audit disallowances and suspensions.
